channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 2 hours.

item.title: 🏴 Australian Defence AI policy risks writing modern EW out of the force | ASPI Strategist
item.description: In 2025, Ukrainian-led opposing forces defeated NATO formations in two major exercises. At exercise Hedgehog in Estonia, about 10 drone operators with cheap first-person-view drones rendered two NATO battalions combat-ineffective in half a day. At ...

item.title: 🏴 Dispatch from Kyiv: Europe steps up on security as the US steps back | Atlantic Council
item.description: European states are now urgently strengthening their militaries and incorporating Ukraine into their defenses. The post Dispatch from Kyiv: Europe steps up on security as the US steps back appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌍 New Iranian Attacks On UAE As Ceasefire Holds By A Thread (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: Joint Chiefs Chairman says Iran attacked U.S. forces 10 times during the ceasefire. The post New Iranian Attacks On UAE As Ceasefire Holds By A Thread (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Navy’s Cahill nominated for SWO boss, Air Force’s Tulley for AMC | Breaking Defense
item.description: Rear Adm. Joseph Cahill is currently the commander of Naval Surface Forces Atlantic.

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon assures safe passage through Strait of Hormuz despite presence of mines | Defense News
item.description: The guarantee follows a security warning from U.S. Naval Forces Central Command to vessels on Monday about the presence of mines in the strait.

item.title: 📍 Britain Leaves Two-Party Politics Behind | Foreign Policy
item.description: Local elections this week will lay bare growing fragmentation across the United Kingdom.

item.title: 🌏 How the War with Iran Is Shaping U.S.-Chinese Competition | War on the Rocks
item.description: The war with Iran has once again raised questions about Washington’s ability to prioritize its interests in East Asia and particularly to manage intensifying competition with Beijing. Furthermore, the war and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz have presented American and…

item.title: 🏴 Policies needed to share AI-generated intel across NATO countries, official says | Defense News
item.description: Currently, the 32 countries that make up the alliance share commercial data through exceptions and workarounds, the NATO official said.

item.title: 📍 The New Critical Minerals Map | Foreign Policy
item.description: Fears of Chinese and U.S. trade retaliation have spawned deals that exclude both powers.

item.title: 🏛️ Navy’s Unwanted Sea Base Ship Will Test At-Sea Rearming Of Destroyer | The War Zone
item.description: At-sea reloading demonstrated by USNS Montford Point could be key in a future fight in the Pacific and give these ships a new lease on life. The post Navy’s Unwanted Sea Base Ship Will Test At-Sea Rearming Of Destroyer appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Turkey Developing Its Own Bladed ‘Ginsu’ Precision Guided Munition | The War Zone
item.description: Turkey is offering a derivative of its MAM-L lightweight munition with the same pop-out, sword-like blades as used on the AGM-114R9X Hellfire. The post Turkey Developing Its Own Bladed ‘Ginsu’ Precision Guided Munition appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Israel eyes dozens of new jets in airpower upgrade | Breaking Defense
item.description: This week on The Break Out, Israel just signaled a notable expansion of air power, and we walk the show floor of the Modern Day Marine conference.

item.title: 🔐 Army turns to ‘hackathons’ to better connect dozens of weapons, systems | Defense One
item.description: Nine leading vendors are to attend the first brainstorming session at Fort Carson, Colorado.

item.title: 🏴 Amb. Ina Marciulionyte on the Future of EU-Mongolia Ties | The Diplomat
item.description: EU projects “prioritize sustainable development, economic development, and democracy in Mongolia” – and, increasingly “peace and security” as well.

item.title: 📍 One-way attack drones under the spotlight at SAHA 2026 | Breaking Defense
item.description: Two naval vessels and three aerial systems, all from Turkish firms, were shown to the public on the first day of the Turkish exposition.

item.title: 🌍 Dispatch from Beirut: Israel-Hezbollah talks risk falling into a failed, decades-long pattern | Atlantic Council
item.description: As the war with Israel revives Hezbollah and divides Lebanon, the ongoing negotiations echo failed 1983 talks. The post Dispatch from Beirut: Israel-Hezbollah talks risk falling into a failed, decades-long pattern appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ US escort of ships through Hormuz is a ‘gift to the world,’ Hegseth says | Defense One
item.description: But secretary says the escorts are temporary, and the world should “step up.”

item.title: 🏛️ As US eyes smaller military footprint in Europe, new unit trains for drone warfare | Defense News
item.description: The U.S. Army is standing up a new unit in Germany to train personnel on drone warfare, even as the Pentagon looks to scale down troop numbers there.

item.title: 🌍 Trump’s Gaza Impasse | Foreign Policy
item.description: Experts say Hamas has few incentives to disarm.

item.title: 🏴 NATO nations size up an interceptor-drone bazaar where low price is everything | Defense News
item.description: A price point advantage is no longer measured in millions of dollars below the competition, but in mere thousands.

item.title: 🌏 North Korea’s Strongwoman-in-Waiting | Foreign Policy
item.description: Kim Jong Un is orchestrating his daughter’s ascent through a campaign of maternal statecraft.

item.title: 🏴 ‘Zeitenwende’ is anchoring Germany’s role as a Baltic Sea Power | Atlantic Council
item.description: The Merz government has been keen to emphasize that Germany is a trustworthy and long-term security provider in the region. The post ‘Zeitenwende’ is anchoring Germany’s role as a Baltic Sea Power appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ 'This is how we prevail in the Pacific': US, allies train to repel amphibious assault | Defense One
item.description: The U.S. Army's 25th Infantry Division brought several new weapons to this year's Balikatan exercise.

item.title: 🌏 Japan’s Arms Export Shift Takes Shape in the Philippines | The Diplomat
item.description: The plan to transfer warships to Manila reflects Tokyo’s new Indo-Pacific security role.

item.title: 🌏 Will the US Serra Verde Acquisition Help Break China’s Rare Earth Monopoly? | The Diplomat
item.description: One of the primary drivers behind the acquisition, backed by the U.S. government, is to disrupt the prevailing Chinese monopoly in rare earths. Will it actually work?

item.title: 🌏 Better Late Than Never: Italy’s Strategic Bet on India | The Diplomat
item.description: Italy’s growing engagement with India reflects a broader, even if somewhat belated, Indo-Pacific turn.

item.title: 🏴 NATO needs policies, standards for sharing AI-enhanced geospatial intel: Official | Breaking Defense
item.description: “The path to AI enabled, allied intelligence advantage runs primarily through governance, not necessarily through additional capability,” said UK Royal Marine Maj. Gen. Paul Lynch, who directs NATO intelligence policy.

item.title: 🌍 Ukraine could lift arms-exports ban this year as would-be buyers line up | Defense News
item.description: Gulf countries threatened by Iran could be some of the first nations to buy Ukrainian air-defense technology at scale.

item.title: 🌍 What the Iran War Reveals About the War Powers Resolution and How Congress Can Act | Just Security
item.description: It is Congress’s responsibility to assert its constitutional prerogatives with respect to the use of force and to rein in a lawless executive. The post What the Iran War Reveals About the War Powers Resolution and How Congress Can Act appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🌏 Taiwan’s President Makes Surprise Trip to Eswatini | The Diplomat
item.description: Lai Ching-te succeeded in making his visit to Taiwan’s lone ally in Africa just weeks after China forced a high-profile cancellation of his original itinerary.
